name: Review Firestore Write Operations
description: Before any file write that touches background.js or popup.js, verify Firestore write patterns follow project standards: merge strategy, error logging format, and dual-write requirement for capsule saves.

then.prompt:
A file write is about to happen in the Synapse AI Link project. If this write touches background.js or popup/popup.js, verify:
1. All setDoc calls to project subcollections use { merge: true } unless it is a full replacement
2. saveCapsule handler writes to BOTH capsules/{id} (flat) AND users/{uid}/projects/{pid}/capsules/{id} (nested) — both paths must be preserved
3. Firestore error logging follows the pattern: console.error('Firestore failure at background.js -> handlerName:', error)
4. getCurrentUserAsync() is used (not auth.currentUser directly) in any service worker function needing the user
If none of these concerns apply to the file being written, approve immediately.
